description: Avatar Behaviour Spec v1 — machine-readable definition of the minimum behaviour vocabulary every registered Live2D model must support. Consumed by video_agent (AvatarCueAgent) to validate cue generation and by authoring agents during model onboarding.

tiers.mandatory: Required for spec compliance. Absence is a hard failure during model onboarding.
tiers.threshold: Required to meet the NPC realism threshold. Absence makes the model appear cheap or limited.
tiers.enhancement: Optional. Adds richness but not required for threshold compliance.

renderer_provided.description: Behaviours the renderer supplies automatically for any compliant model. video_agent must NOT emit explicit cues for these — they run unconditionally.

behaviours.description: CubismEyeBlink fires every 3–6 s when the model has an EyeBlink parameter group.
Sinusoidal breathing-driven head sway on AngleX/Y/Z/BodyAngleX. Always active.
Body breathing cycle driven by the same CubismBreath instance as head drift.
Expression transitions use fade in/out timing from .exp3.json files. No snaps.
Idle_0 motion restarts automatically whenever no higher-priority motion is playing.

expressions.label: neutral
expressions.semantic: Default resting state. Calm and composed. Use as a reset between other expressions.
expressions.when_to_use: Scene start, pauses, topic transitions, any moment without strong emotional valence.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: happy
expressions.semantic: Warm positive emotion. Elevated joy — more intense than a neutral smile.
expressions.when_to_use: Good news, laughter, warm moments, affirmation, sharing something exciting.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: serious
expressions.semantic: Focus or emphasis. Not angry — deliberate concentration or gravity.
expressions.when_to_use: Important points, warnings, instructions, moments requiring authority.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.
expressions.note: majo maps this to an authored .exp3.json that does not yet exist. Must be authored during majo spec build.

expressions.label: surprised
expressions.semantic: Shock or astonishment. Sudden unexpected information.
expressions.when_to_use: Unexpected reveals, sudden facts, startled reactions.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: sad
expressions.when_to_use: Bad news, losses, empathetic listening, regret.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: angry
expressions.semantic: Frustration, irritation, or confrontation. Strong negative emotion.
expressions.when_to_use: Injustice, strong disagreement, frustration with a situation (not the viewer).

facial_regions.mouth: Tight downturned or open with tension

expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: bored
expressions.semantic: Disengagement or mild disdain. Heavy-lidded uninterested look.
expressions.when_to_use: Sarcasm, impatience, obvious statements, comedic deflation.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: curious
expressions.semantic: Mild interest or attentiveness. Lighter than surprise.
expressions.when_to_use: Listening beats, gentle reactions, rhetorical questions.
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

expressions.label: embarrassed
expressions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo behaviour authoring is complete.

reactions.label: idle
reactions.semantic: Default ambient loop. Breathing, sway, subtle head drift, ambient gaze variation.
reactions.when_to_use: Runs automatically. Do not emit explicit idle cues — the renderer manages idle restart.
reactions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo idle motion quality is verified.

reactions.label: nod
reactions.semantic: Discourse acknowledgement. Signals agreement or sentence completion. Highest-ROI single reaction.
reactions.when_to_use: Sentence boundaries in speech, affirmations, agreement beats, listening acknowledgements.
reactions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo nod motion is authored and approved.

reactions.label: look_away
reactions.semantic: Deliberate gaze break. Conveys thinking, recalling, or internal processing. Breaks camera lock.
reactions.when_to_use: Recall moments ('let me think...'), rhetorical pauses, moments of internal reflection.
reactions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo look_away motion is authored and approved.
reactions.note: Must use smooth keyframe curves on ParamEyeBallX/Y — not a raw gaze cue snap. Head follow (AngleY) keyframes should be offset ~150 ms after eye movement and at ~30–40% amplitude.

reactions.label: tap
reactions.semantic: Startled or responsive jolt. Reaction to an unexpected interruption or touch.
reactions.when_to_use: Sudden topic changes, startled reactions, comedic timing beats.
reactions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo tap motion is authored and approved.

reactions.label: shake
reactions.semantic: Negative head shake. Signals disagreement or refusal.
reactions.video_todo: Add video clip once majo shake motion is authored and approved.

emotion.description: Blend to a named facial expression using CubismExpressionMotionManager. Transition is smooth — fade in/out timing comes from the .exp3.json file.
emotion.notes: Always emit a 'neutral' cue at scene start (t=0). Always return to 'neutral' after a strong expression unless the next line continues the same emotion.

reaction.description: Trigger a pre-built motion clip. Plays once at the given time, then the model returns to idle.
reaction.notes: Nod at sentence boundaries. look_away at thinking/recall moments. Do not stack reactions — allow previous reaction to complete before triggering another.

gaze.description: Eye direction override via SetParameterValue — snaps instantly, no interpolation. Use only for held static positions.
gaze.notes: Do NOT use gaze cues for smooth transitions — they snap. Use the look_away reaction instead. Reserve raw gaze cues for held directional positions (e.g. consistently looking at an off-screen object).

head.description: Head angle override in degrees via SetParameterValue — snaps instantly. Interaction with CubismBreath is unverified; prefer reaction clips for head movement.
head.notes: Use with caution. CubismBreath writes to the same AngleX/Y/Z parameters and may overwrite cue values. Smooth head movement should be authored into reaction clips, not driven by raw head cues.

motion_file_requirements.parameter_source: model .cdi3.json — never copied from another model

idle_motion_requirements.notes: Must produce visible head movement. CubismBreath adds drift on top; idle motion must not hold a fixed target that fights the breath layer.

versioning.changes: Initial spec: 4 mandatory + 2 threshold expressions; idle + 3 threshold reactions; gaze via upstream cues only.
